element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns fundamental combat mechanics against initial Skinhead enemies
Baseline threat to establish baseline combat rhythm
Enhances combat range and damage; players must learn weapon timing
Introduces need for spacing and defensive positioning
Requires longer approach distances and evasion patterns
Mobile threat that forces player to predict movement and timing
Ranged projectile threat requiring evasion and cover awareness
Punishes close-range contact; encourages spacing discipline
Final confrontation integrating all learned mechanics

typical_relief_triggers: Clear arena space before next enemy wave
typical_tension_triggers: Loss of weapon (dropping nunchaku when hit)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave-based tension with escalating enemy density and weapon variety; early stages establish breathing room, mid-late stages compress space and introduce multiple simultaneous threats

difficulty_curve.early_game: Street stage introduces basic mechanics with manageable Skinhead density; punches/kicks adequate for progression
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Junkyard and Brooklyn Bridge stages add armed enemies (knives, chains) and motorbikes; nunchaku becomes essential tool; enemy spacing tightens
difficulty_curve.late_game: Back street and construction building introduce gun-wielding enemies and grapple threats; requires mastery of all defensive and offensive mechanics
notable_spikes: Introduction of chain-wielding enemies (increased reach threat)
Motorbike enemies requiring prediction and timing
Gun enemies forcing ranged evasion patterns

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Stage progression by environment and threat type
notable_level_patterns.description: Five stages present distinct urban locations (street → junkyard → bridge → alley → construction). Each introduces new enemy archetypes while maintaining horizontal beat-em-up flow. Enemies graduate from unarmed → melee weapons → mobile/ranged threats.

Stages structured around successive waves of enemies with escalating complexity. Early waves feature single enemy types; later waves mix weapon variants and attack styles, forcing tactical adaptation.
Nunchaku pickups appear strategically during stages. Loss of weapon on hit creates tension—players must weigh aggressive positioning against defensive recovery.

NYC locations (street, junkyard, Brooklyn Bridge, back street, construction site) provide thematic progression without altering core mechanics. Progression feels natural but gameplay remains consistent.
